title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with flavor!

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cream and cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian favorite.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian curry featuring succulent chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This classic recipe delivers a perfect balance of tangy, sweet, and mildly spiced flavors, making it a truly comforting and indulgent dish. Prepare to savor the authentic taste of Delhi with every delicious bite.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken breast or thigh

ingredients.notes: from fresh or canned crushed tomatoes
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken cubes with plain yogurt, 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Sear the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook and Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, coriander powder, cumin powder, and remaining 1/2 tsp red chili powder. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate. Add the soaked cashews and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a simmer, then remove from heat. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash more water if it is too thick.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Pour the smooth sauce back into the pan. Bring it to a gentle simmer over low-medium heat. Add the seared chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir gently to combine and cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the sauce. If the sauce is too thick, add a little water or chicken stock to reach your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Stir in the remaining 2 tbsp butter, heavy cream, sugar or honey, and the crushed Kasuri Methi. Mix well and cook for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring the sauce is heated through but not boiled vigorously after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt and sweetness if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For a smoky flavor, you can grill or broil the marinated chicken before adding it to the sauce, mimicking tandoor cooking.
Always soak cashews for at least 15 minutes in warm water to ensure a silky-smooth sauce when blended. For a nut-free version, use sunflower seeds or coconut cream.
Avoid boiling the sauce vigorously after adding cream, as it can curdle. Simmer gently over low heat for a rich, smooth finish. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in India as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a global culinary phenomenon. This iconic Indian chicken recipe has captured hearts worldwide with its rich, creamy, and subtly spiced tomato-based gravy, paired with tender, succulent pieces of chicken. Originating in Delhi in the 1950s, Butter Chicken quickly became a staple, renowned for its comforting flavors that appeal to both seasoned spice lovers and those new to Indian cuisine. If you're searching for the ultimate Butter Chicken Recipe that delivers authentic taste and restaurant-quality results right in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every step of creating this beloved classic, ensuring a truly unforgettable meal.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?
At its core, Murgh Makhani translates to "chicken with butter," a name that perfectly encapsulates its indulgent nature. This dish features chicken pieces, typically marinated in yogurt and spices, then traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) or pan-fried until slightly charred. The magic truly happens with the gravy: a luscious blend of tomatoes, cream, butter, and a delicate balance of aromatic spices like ginger, garlic, and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). Unlike many spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken is characterized by its mild heat and a harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and savory notes, making it incredibly comforting and universally appealing. It's a testament to the art of balancing flavors, and mastering the classic Butter Chicken Recipe is a rewarding culinary journey.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken
Creating an authentic Butter Chicken Recipe requires a selection of fresh, quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces (thighs are recommended for juiciness)
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Gravy:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter, plus more for cooking
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped (optional, for deeper flavor)
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 kg ripe tomatoes, pureed (or 2 cans 400g crushed tomatoes)
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, then drained)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri preferred)
*   1 tsp cumin powder
*   1 tsp coriander powder
*   1 tbsp kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1-2 tsp sugar or honey (to balance acidity)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream, plus extra for garnish
*   Salt to taste
*   Water, as needed

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Butter Chicken
Follow this detailed Butter Chicken Recipe to achieve culinary perfection:

1.  Marinate the Chicken: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

2.  Cook the Chicken: Heat 1 tbsp oil and 1 tbsp butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer (cook in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding). Cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

3.  Prepare the Gravy Base: In the same pan, add another tablespoon of butter. If using, add chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  Build the Flavor: Add the tomato puree, soaked cashews, turmeric powder, red chili powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Stir well. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes are soft and the oil starts to separate.

5.  Blend the Gravy: Carefully transfer the cooked gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

6.  Finish the Gravy: Return the pan with the strained gravy to medium-low heat. Add the crushed kasoori methi, garam masala, sugar or honey, and salt to taste. Stir well and simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

7.  Combine and Serve: Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the heavy cream and gently mix. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until the chicken is heated through and the gravy is warmed. Do not boil after adding cream, as it can curdle. Garnish with a swirl of extra cream and fresh coriander leaves.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
These tips will elevate your Butter Chicken Recipe from good to unforgettable:

*   Marination is Key: The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination truly makes a difference.
*   Chicken Thighs vs. Breast: While breast meat works, chicken thighs remain juicier and more flavorful, especially after cooking.
*   Smooth Gravy: Blending the gravy thoroughly and then straining it is crucial for that characteristic silky-smooth texture. Don't skip this step for an authentic experience.
*   Kasoori Methi: Lightly toast the dried fenugreek leaves in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing them between your palms. This releases their full aroma and distinct flavor, which is essential to Murgh Makhani.
*   Good Quality Butter: Since butter is a star ingredient, use high-quality unsalted butter for the best flavor.
*   Balance Sweetness and Acidity: Taste your gravy and adjust the sugar/honey and salt as needed. The sweetness helps balance the tanginess of the tomatoes.
*   Don't Overcook Cream: Add cream at the very end and only heat through gently to prevent it from curdling.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani
Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments. For a truly authentic Indian dining experience, serve your Murgh Makhani with warm, fluffy naan bread (garlic naan or butter naan are excellent choices) to scoop up every last bit of that delectable gravy. It also pairs wonderfully with steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice). A simple side salad of sliced cucumber, onions, and tomatoes, perhaps with a squeeze of lemon, provides a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry. Don't forget a garnish of fresh chopped coriander and a final swirl of cream for presentation and added richness.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
While the classic Butter Chicken Recipe is a masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary preferences or to introduce new flavors:

*   Vegetarian Alternative: For a meat-free version, substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Paneer Makhani is a very popular dish in itself.
*   Healthier Options: To reduce richness, you can use less butter and cream, or substitute some of the cream with milk (though this will alter the texture slightly). Some recipes use cashew paste to thicken the gravy instead of relying heavily on cream.
*   Spicier Kick: If you prefer more heat, add an extra green chili or a pinch of regular red chili powder along with the Kashmiri chili powder.
*   Smoky Flavor: For an authentic tandoori-style smoky flavor, you can briefly "smoke" the finished dish. Place a small piece of burning charcoal in a small metal bowl inside the pot, drizzle a little ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for a few minutes.
*   Nut-Free Option: If you have nut allergies, you can substitute the cashews with a tablespoon of poppy seeds or melon seeds (soaked and blended), or simply omit them and rely on the tomatoes and cream for texture.

Armed with this comprehensive Butter Chicken Recipe, you're ready to recreate the magic of Murgh Makhani in your own kitchen. This classic Indian chicken recipe is a celebration of flavor and texture, promising a meal that is both comforting and exquisitely delicious. Enjoy the process, savor the aroma, and delight in the rich taste of homemade Butter Chicken.
